title: Phase 5: Pre-turn context enrichment
description:
Implement pre-turn context enrichment.

FILE: /home/wirebot/focusa/crates/focusa-core/src/expression/engine.rs
WHERE: In assemble_from() function or new pre-assembly step

FAST PATH (inline, ≤50ms, no LLM):
  1. Extract keywords from raw_user_input (split by spaces, filter stopwords)
  2. Parallel async fetch:
     a. wb wiki search '$KEYWORDS' --format json --limit 3 (50ms timeout)
     b. HTTP GET :8200/v1/search?query=$KEYWORDS&limit=5 (50ms timeout)
  3. Merge results into AssemblyInput alongside existing context
  4. On timeout → proceed with whatever state is already cached

DEEP PATH (async from PREVIOUS turn, background):
  In workers after each turn, generate anticipatory queries:
    POST to MiniMax: 'Based on this conversation, what will the user likely ask next?
    Return 3 search queries for wiki and memory retrieval.'
  Cache results in FocusaState.anticipated_context field
  Next turn's Expression Engine checks anticipated_context first

VERIFY: Prompt assembly includes wiki/Mem0 results relevant to current input.

title: Phase 5: Snapshot thinning
description:
Implement snapshot thinning.
FILE: /data/wirebot/bin/focusa-nightly-hygiene.sh (step 2)
SQL: 
  -- Keep every mutation for 24h
  -- Keep daily snapshots for 30d  
  -- Keep weekly for 90d
  -- Keep monthly after
  DELETE FROM snapshots 
  WHERE timestamp < datetime('now','-1 day') 
  AND rowid NOT IN (
    SELECT MAX(rowid) FROM snapshots WHERE timestamp >= datetime('now','-30 days') GROUP BY date(timestamp)
    UNION
    SELECT MAX(rowid) FROM snapshots WHERE timestamp >= datetime('now','-90 days') GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%W', timestamp)
    UNION
    SELECT MAX(rowid) FROM snapshots WHERE timestamp < datetime('now','-90 days') GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', timestamp)
  );
VERIFY: Snapshot table size reduces. Recent snapshots preserved.
REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10.2

title: Phase 1: Context Core to Focusa constraint injection
description:
Inject Context Core operator state into Focusa every turn.

FILE: /home/wirebot/focusa/crates/focusa-core/src/runtime/daemon.rs
WHERE: In process_action() for TurnStarted events, or in the proxy request handler

IMPLEMENTATION:
  1. Add reqwest HTTP client to daemon (or use existing if present)
  2. On each turn start, call GET http://127.0.0.1:7400/me with 2s timeout
  3. Parse response JSON for: state.mode, state.interruptibility, timely.circadian_phase, 
     timely.is_quiet_hours, rescuetime.productivity_score, policy.agent_should
  4. Map to Focus State constraints:
     - interruptibility=very_low → add constraint 'Do not ask questions, queue them'
     - is_quiet_hours=true → add constraint 'Operator may be sleeping, be concise'
     - agent_should=queue_questions → add constraint 'Queue questions, do not interrupt'
  5. Store in FocusaState as operator_modulation field (new field)
  6. Expression Engine reads operator_modulation and injects into prompt

CONTEXT CORE RESPONSE SHAPE (from live check):
  {state: {mode, interruptibility}, timely: {circadian_phase, is_quiet_hours}, 
   policy: {agent_should, ask_user_now}, rescuetime: {productivity_score}}

FALLBACK: On timeout/error, use last-known state with TTL (cache for 5 min)
VERIFY: wb focusa status shows operator state. Prompt assembly includes operator constraints.
DEPENDS: Focusa daemon running, Context Core running (:7400)
REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §9.5
notes:
CROSS-REF: Pi extension (apps/pi-extension) injects Context Core state via /wbm (doc 44 §29). This bead covers the Focusa daemon-side integration with Context Core for ALL agents, not just Pi.
DONE: Context Core queried on every proxy turn. Operator state (interruptibility, quiet hours, policy) injected as Focus State constraints. 2s timeout, graceful fallback on failure. Added to chat_completions handler in proxy.rs.

title: Spec gap: wire CacheStore into FocusaState + cache status/events endpoints
description:
docs/18-cache-permission-matrix.md (AUTHORITATIVE) + docs/19-intentional-cache-busting.md (AUTHORITATIVE)

SPEC REQUIREMENTS:

docs/18 §1 Cache Classes: C0 Immutable (safe), C1 Deterministic Assembly (conditional), C2 Ephemeral Compute (volatile), C3 Provider KV (opportunistic), C4 Forbidden.
docs/18 §2 Cache Permission Matrix: 10 components × 5 classes with ✅/⚠️/❌/⛔ ratings.
docs/18 §3 Cache Key Requirements: agent_id, constitution_version, model_id, harness_id, focus_state_revision, token_budget, retrieval_policy_version. "If any required key field is missing → caching is disallowed."
docs/18 §6 Hard Invalidation Rules: Agent ID changed, Constitution version changed, Model/harness changed, Focus State revision changed, Focus Stack push/pop, Gate threshold changed, Token budget changed, Tool schemas changed, New high-priority artifact, Task authority changed.
docs/18 §7 Observability: "Every cached lookup MUST emit: hit/miss, cache class, key hash, invalidation reason, compute time saved estimate."

docs/19 §1 Bust Trigger Categories: A (Fresh Evidence), B (Authority Boundary), C (Compaction), D (Staleness Risk), E (Salience Collapse), F (Provider Mismatch).
docs/19 §3 CLI: focusa cache status, focusa cache bust --reason=<...>, focusa cache policy show.

CODE STATUS:
- CacheClass enum (C0-C4) — EXISTS in types.rs
- CacheBustCategory enum (A-F) — EXISTS in types.rs
- cache/mod.rs — 196 LOC, has CacheStore struct + invalidation methods + tests

MISSING:
- CacheStore not in FocusaState (not persisted, not accessible to API)
- No /v1/cache/status or /v1/cache/events endpoints
- No bust triggers fire from daemon (spec §6 hard invalidation on Focus State revision, stack push/pop, etc.)
- No cache key construction from mandatory fields
- No observability events (hit/miss/bust) emitted
- No CLI commands (cache status/bust/policy)

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Cache busting fires on Focus State revision change, stack push/pop, gate threshold change
- Every cached lookup emits hit/miss with cache class and key hash
- focusa cache status shows cache state
- If caching and cognition disagree, cognition wins
notes: CacheStore wired into daemon. Bust triggers on stack push/pop (AuthorityChange) and FocusStateUpdated (FreshEvidence). CacheStore implementation already had put/get/bust/clear + 4 tests. 135 tests pass. Committed: 0d72cbe.

title: Spec gap: contribution pipeline enforcement (queue approvals + submit tracking)
description:
docs/22-data-contribution.md (AUTHORITATIVE)

SPEC REQUIREMENTS:

§0 Canonical Principle: "Users contribute meaning, not surveillance. Focusa never uploads raw conversations, silent telemetry, or private intent."
§3 Opt-In Lifecycle: "Contribution is OFF by default. User must explicitly enable via onboarding prompt, CLI command, or UI toggle. No silent defaults. No dark patterns."
§3.2 Contribution Policy: enabled, dataset_types, min_uxp, max_ufi, min_autonomy_level, exclude_domains, require_manual_review, upload_schedule, network_policy, power_policy, redaction_level, consent_version.
§4 Eligibility: contribution enabled AND export_allowed AND Focus State complete AND CLT lineage intact AND no secrets AND license valid AND UXP >= threshold AND UFI <= threshold. "If uncertain → exclude."
§5 Allowed: Focus State snapshots, CLT summaries, structured tool outputs, reducer state transitions, preference/correction signals, autonomy metrics. Forbidden: raw conversations, raw prompts, system messages, provider fingerprints, secrets, personal identifiers.
§6 Local Queue: inspectable, approve/reject/redact/pause/purge.
§7 Background Export Worker: async, never blocks cognition, respects schedule/power/network, rate-limited, resumable, idempotent, encrypted.
§9 CLI: focusa contribute status/enable/pause/review/policy edit/export-now/purge. "CLI must show queue size, last upload, active policy. Never auto-enable."

CODE STATUS:
- ContributionState/Policy types — EXISTS
- enqueue_contribution() / approve_contribution() / submit_approved() — EXISTS in training/mod.rs

MISSING:
- CLI focusa contribute commands not wired
- No queue persistence (in-memory only)
- No eligibility filter (spec §4 conditions not checked)
- No background export worker
- No policy enforcement (min_uxp, max_ufi, exclude_domains checks)
- No encryption
- No upload destination (sink) implementation
- contribution.enabled always false with no path to enable via CLI/API

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- focusa contribute status shows queue size, last upload, active policy
- focusa contribute enable turns on contribution
- Eligible items appear in queue after turns
- Ineligible items (PII, low UXP, high UFI) excluded
- focusa contribute review shows inspectable queue
- No opt-in → no export
notes: Contribution API: enable/pause/approve/submit endpoints. Core logic: enqueue/approve/submit. Policy type exists. Committed: ac5fb5d.

title: Phase 2: Procedural memory seeding from Wiki skills
description:

FILE: /home/wirebot/focusa/crates/focusa-core/src/runtime/daemon.rs

ALL items tagged: source_type: 'pi_session', source:pi, surface:pi

title: Phase 5: Post-turn evaluation (async)
description:
Implement post-turn evaluation (async at R0, inline at R1+).

FILE: /home/wirebot/focusa/crates/focusa-core/src/runtime/daemon.rs
WHERE: After TurnCompleted event processing

R0 (ASYNC, after response sent):
  Spawn background task:
    POST to MiniMax M2.7: 'Evaluate this response. Did it answer the question? Is it consistent with these prior decisions? Does it violate these constraints? Return JSON {answers_question, consistent, violates_constraints[], confidence, quality_notes}'
    Input: operator question + model response + active constraints + prior decisions
    Budget: ≤300 tokens, 2s timeout
  On result:
    If constraint violations → add to Focus State failures for next turn
    If quality low → add note to Focus State: 'Previous response may need correction'
  On failure: skip evaluation silently

R1+ (INLINE, before response sent):
  Run RFM microcell validators in parallel (separate bead focusa-n7w)
  If fail → regenerate once

EVERY 3RD TURN at R0 (sampling):
  let should_evaluate = state.telemetry.total_events % 3 == 0;

VERIFY: After turn with constraint violation, next turn's Focus State shows quality flag.
DEPENDS: focusa-e2h (workers upgraded for LLM calls — reuse HTTP client)
REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §11.8

title: Pipeline fix: build Pi session JSONL parser for extraction
description:

PI SESSION FORMAT: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<dir>/*.jsonl

  111 files, 1.7GB total.

     e. For each chunk: POST :8100/v1/memory/extract-conversation
        {user_message: chunk_users, assistant_message: chunk_assistants, surface: 'pi-session'}

  4. Rate: 2 sessions per nightly run, 2s between chunks
  
NOTE: Live Pi extraction handled by /wbm extension (focusa-6dm). This is ONE-TIME backfill only.
After 111 files processed (~56 nights), retire this script.

title: Phase 2: Mem0 session-start seeding into Focusa
description:
Seed Focusa semantic memory from Mem0 on session start.

FILE: /home/wirebot/focusa/crates/focusa-core/src/runtime/daemon.rs
WHERE: In run() method, after session start, or in translate_action for StartSession

IMPLEMENTATION:
  1. On SessionStarted event, derive search query from active focus intent + project
  2. HTTP GET http://127.0.0.1:8200/v1/search?query=$INTENT&user_id=wirebot_verious&limit=10
     Timeout: 3s
  3. For each returned memory:
     state.memory.semantic.insert(format!('mem0.{}', memory.id), SemanticRecord {
       key: format!('mem0.{}', memory.id),
       value: memory.text,
       source: 'mem0',
       confidence: memory.score,
       ttl: Some(Duration::hours(24)),  // Session-scoped, expires if not refreshed
       ...
     })
  4. These seeded memories are available to Expression Engine for prompt assembly

FALLBACK: On Mem0 timeout/error → skip seeding, log warning, continue with local memory only

description:

  1. Add HTTP client call to MiniMax M2.7 via OpenClaw gateway

     Body: {model: 'minimax/MiniMax-M2.7', messages: [{role:'user', content: prompt}], max_tokens: 500}

     DetectRepetition: 'Is this content semantically repetitive compared to recent context? Return {is_repetitive, ratio, evidence}'
     ScanForErrors: 'Identify errors, stack traces, and failure patterns. Return {errors: [{type, severity, context}]}'
     SuggestMemory: 'Extract stable facts, preferences, and behavioral patterns worth remembering. Return {suggestions: [{text, confidence}]}'

  4. Parse JSON response. On parse failure → fall back to regex.

BUILD: cargo build --release (needs reqwest or similar HTTP client added to Cargo.toml)
TEST: cargo test + integration test sending real content through each worker

title: Pi: per-LLM-call context injection via context event
description:
Inject live Focus State per LLM call via context event.

FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts
HOOK: context (fires before EVERY LLM call, not just first)

IMPLEMENTATION:
  1. Check focusaEnabled flag
  2. Calculate available headroom: ctx.getContextUsage() (§36.7)
     maxFocusaTokens = min(max(headroom * 0.2, 200), 2000)
  3. GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/ascc/state (cached 5s TTL, 2s timeout)
  4. Truncate state to maxFocusaTokens
  5. Build message: {role: 'user', content: [{type: 'text', text: '[FOCUSA STATE]\nFrame: ...\nDecisions: ...\nConstraints: ...'}], timestamp: Date.now()}
  6. Return { messages: [focusaMsg, ...event.messages] }
  7. On Focusa timeout: skip injection, use cached if available

NOTE: This is the SECONDARY injection (live state refresh).
PRIMARY is before_agent_start (behavioral instructions, §35.2).
Per §36.6 layering rules.

VERIFY: Multi-tool turn (5 tool calls). Each LLM call includes updated Focus State.
DEPENDS: Extension skeleton, Focusa running.
REF: doc 44 §33.2, §36.6, §36.7

title: Phase 0: Implement nightly hygiene loop
description:
Create nightly hygiene script with 9 steps.

CREATE: /data/wirebot/bin/focusa-nightly-hygiene.sh
CREATE: /etc/systemd/system/focusa-hygiene.timer (OnCalendar=*-*-* 04:00:00)
CREATE: /etc/systemd/system/focusa-hygiene.service (Type=oneshot)

SCRIPT CONTENTS (9 steps):
  1. Event log compact: call /data/wirebot/bin/focusa-compact-events.sh (from focusa-s95)
  2. Snapshot thin: 
     sqlite3 focusa.sqlite 'DELETE FROM snapshots WHERE timestamp < datetime("now","-24 hours") AND rowid NOT IN (SELECT MAX(rowid) FROM snapshots GROUP BY date(timestamp));'
  3. Session prune: wb session prune --budget 512KB
  4. Procedural rules: curl -X POST :8787/v1/memory/decay (or direct SQLite if no endpoint)
     Then remove weight < 0.01: UPDATE procedural SET status='removed' WHERE weight < 0.01
  5. Semantic TTL: check TTL field, remove expired
  6. Mem0 dedupe: POST :8200/v1/deduplicate (if endpoint exists) or manual scan
  7. Wiki tier demotion: call wiki-agent tier scan or direct GraphQL query
  8. CLT compact: if CLT nodes > 1000, compact (requires Focusa code change — skip in v1)
  9. VACUUM: sqlite3 focusa.sqlite 'VACUUM;'

VERIFY: After run, ls -lh focusa.sqlite shows size reduced. No data corruption.
DEPENDS: focusa-s95 (event compaction script must exist first)
REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10.8

§5 Token Types: Owner (full, rotatable), Agent (scoped, revocable), Integration (read-only, expirable).
§6 Policy Interaction: "Permissions are necessary but not sufficient. Even with permission: Focus Gate may block, Autonomy level may prevent action, Contribution policy may deny export, Constitution rules may override. Policy always wins over permission."
§7 Auditing: "Every denied or allowed command logs: token_id, agent_id, permission checked, outcome, reason."

CODE STATUS:

- Auth middleware checking FOCUSA_AUTH_TOKEN env var — EXISTS (auth.rs, 52 LOC)
- Permissions route — EXISTS (permissions.rs, 93 LOC)
- ApiToken type with token_id/token_type/scopes/created_at/expires_at/revoked — EXISTS
- PermissionScope type with domain/action — EXISTS

MISSING:

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Every API request is authenticated

- Commands require explicit permission
- Lack of permission → 403 forbidden
- Every denied or allowed command logs: token_id, agent_id, permission, outcome, reason

Implemented: TokenStore wired into AppState, token CRUD API (/v1/tokens/create, /revoke, /list), AuditEntry + AuditLog types with record_audit(), auth middleware (bearer token validation), permission_context + forbid helpers used by gate/capabilities routes. 135 tests pass. Committed: af8b510.

title: Focusa cognitive loop: spec-faithful wiring [AUDIT 2026-03-30]
description:
Audit on 2026-03-30 found Focusa core is ~45% spec-faithful. Architecture, types, reducer are correct. But the cognitive loop — the thing that makes Focusa think — has two severed tendons:

1. SIGNAL → CANDIDATE PIPELINE: 14,659 signals accumulated, 0 candidates created. aggregate_signals() exists but is never called. The 5-step Focus Gate algorithm (G1-detail-06) is not wired into the daemon loop.

2. TURN → WORKER TRIGGER: Worker infrastructure exists (receiver, queue, executor, result handler) but nothing ever sends Action::WorkerEnqueue. Background cognition plane is dormant.

Downstream effects: Focus State on all frames is empty (all 10 ASCC slots blank). Expression Engine assembles prompts with no cognitive context. Autonomy scoring has 0 observations. ASCC checkpoints never created. UFI signals never detected.

This epic tracks all missing wiring to bring Focusa core to spec-faithful operation.

title: Signal → Candidate pipeline: wire aggregate_signals into daemon loop
description:
G1-detail-06 "Focus Gate Algorithm (MVP Deterministic Heuristics)" specifies a 5-step pipeline that is not wired into the daemon event loop.

SPEC REQUIREMENTS (exact):

Step 1: Normalize signals — On ingest: if payload_ref missing and payload is large -> store to ECS -> set payload_ref. Derive tags (error class, file path hints, tool name hints). Create fingerprint for dedupe: hash(kind + normalized summary + frame_context + key tags).

Step 2: Candidate matching or creation — If fingerprint matches existing candidate: increment times_seen, update last_seen_at, increase pressure by Δp. Else: create new candidate with base pressure.

Step 3: Pressure update rules — Additive base increments: user_input +0.6, tool_output +0.5, assistant_output +0.2, warning +0.7, error +1.2, repeated_pattern +0.8, manual_pin +2.0. Modifiers: goal alignment (related_frame == active: ×1.3, in stack path: ×1.1, else ×0.8), recency (within last 5 min: +0.3), risk (error/warning: +0.4). Suppression: if suppressed_until in future, do not surface. Decay: periodic tick pressure *= 0.98 for non-manual candidates.

Step 4: Surfacing — Candidates surfaced when pressure >= SURFACE_THRESHOLD (default 2.2) AND not suppressed AND not resolved. Surfacing emits gate.candidate_surfaced event. Returns candidate in API/UI lists.

Step 5: User actions — suppress, resolve, or convert to frame push.

CODE STATUS:
- base_pressure() matches spec values — EXISTS
- decay_candidates() with 0.98 factor, skips pinned — EXISTS
- surfaced_candidates() threshold filter — EXISTS
- aggregate_signals() groups by (kind, frame_context), calculates cumulative_pressure — EXISTS, ZERO CALL SITES
- suggest_candidate_kind() maps groups to CandidateKind — EXISTS, ZERO CALL SITES
- Steps 1-4 as active pipeline in daemon loop — MISSING
- Fingerprint dedup — MISSING
- Pressure modifiers (goal alignment ×1.3/×1.1/×0.8, recency +0.3, risk +0.4) — MISSING
- Signal Vec unbounded (14,659 signals, no cap) — spec says "Keep last N candidates (default 200)" for candidates; signals need equivalent bound

PERSISTENCE (spec): "Persist candidate list with bounded size. Keep last N candidates (default 200). Persist to ~/.focusa/state/focus_gate.json"

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Duplicate signals merge into same candidate
- Pressure increases predictably with repeated occurrences
- Suppression hides candidate for the set period
- Decay reduces stale candidates over time
- No candidate action changes focus stack without explicit CLI/API call
notes: Implemented run_gate_pipeline() with full 5-step algorithm. 7 new tests (14 total gate tests), 113 total pass. Signal cap 1000, candidate cap 200. Committed: 731a21c.

title: Focus State population: enrich FocusStateDelta from turn content
description:
G1-07-ascc "Update Pipeline (MVP)" specifies that when a new turn arrives, delta content is summarized into structured slots and merged into existing checkpoint. This pipeline is not implemented.

SPEC REQUIREMENTS (exact):

Update Pipeline inputs: frame_id, turn_id, raw_user_input (small), assistant_output (small or handle), tool_outputs (handles), events relevant to this frame, optionally extracted facts/preferences from worker.

Delta Summarization Rule: 1) Determine delta content = only new items since last anchor. 2) Summarize delta into structured slots. 3) Merge into existing checkpoint using deterministic merge rules.

MVP summarization mechanism: "can be LLM-assisted OR rule-based. MVP can start with rule-based extraction (regex for file paths/errors) plus optional call to a cheap summarizer model if configured (NOT required)."

Spec requires: "You must implement a pluggable summarizer interface: Summarizer::summarize_delta(existing_checkpoint, delta_input) -> delta_sections"

Merge Rules are implemented in focus/state.rs apply_delta() — CORRECT. But apply_delta() is only ever called with current_state = token count string. All other slots permanently empty.

Specific merge rule gap — open_questions: spec says "if question is answered in delta, remove it (simple match heuristic)". Code only appends, never removes answered questions.

ASCC must also update: revision += 1, anchor_turn_id = turn_id, updated_at = now. Emit event: ascc.delta_applied.

CODE STATUS:
- FocusState struct: 10 slots matching spec — EXISTS
- FocusStateDelta struct: all 10 optional fields — EXISTS
- apply_delta() with caps (30/50/30/20/15/10/20/20): dedup, append, replace, prepend — EXISTS, CORRECT
- extract_ascc_delta() worker: heuristic extraction of decisions/next_steps/constraints/failures — EXISTS, NEVER CALLED
- Proxy FocusStateDelta on TurnCompleted: only sets current_state to "Turn <id>: X prompt + Y completion tokens" — INSUFFICIENT
- Pluggable Summarizer interface — MISSING
- open_questions remove-when-answered heuristic — MISSING
- ascc.delta_applied event emission — MISSING (FocusStateUpdated is emitted but not ascc.delta_applied)

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Delta updates never overwrite unrelated slots
- Anchors advance monotonically
- Prompt serialization stable and bounded
- Large tool outputs become handles (never inline)
notes: Implemented: broadened extract_ascc_delta (10 slots), proxy delta enrichment (both paths), open_questions remove-when-answered, apply_worker_result 8 fields. 114 tests pass. Committed: 2df5a8d.

title: ASCC CheckpointRecord lifecycle: instantiation, revision tracking, persistence
description:
G1-07-ascc "Structured Checkpoint Schema (MVP)" defines CheckpointRecord with fields: frame_id, revision (u64), updated_at, anchor_turn_id (last processed turn), sections (AsccSections), breadcrumbs (Vec<HandleRef>), confidence (optional, MVP can omit), history (Vec<AsccDeltaMeta>, bounded, optional).

The spec states: "Checkpoint per frame stored in: ~/.focusa/ascc/<frame_id>.json. MVP: only current checkpoint required."

The spec states ASCC provides two serializers: to_string_compact() and to_messages_slots() for prompt assembly.

The UPDATE section adds per-slot metadata: pinned (bool), last_updated_at. And ASCC must expose to_digest() for ultra-compact fallback when prompt budget cannot be satisfied.

CODE STATUS:
- CheckpointRecord struct in types.rs — EXISTS, NEVER INSTANTIATED (zero usage outside type definition)
- AsccSections struct in types.rs — EXISTS, NEVER INSTANTIATED
- ascc_checkpoint_id on FrameRecord — always None, never set
- ~/.focusa/ascc/ directory — DOES NOT EXIST
- to_string_compact() — MISSING
- to_messages_slots() — MISSING
- to_digest() — MISSING (build_focus_digest in expression engine is a local workaround, not the spec interface)
- Per-slot pinned/last_updated_at metadata — MISSING
- Pluggable Summarizer interface — MISSING

FocusState on FrameRecord is being used as a de-facto ASCC substitute (same 10 slots), but lacks: revision tracking, anchor_turn_id, breadcrumbs, history, serializers, per-slot pinning, persistence as separate file.

SPEC INVARIANTS:
- "ASCC degradation is explicit"
- "ASCC never silently truncates pinned sections"

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Delta updates never overwrite unrelated slots
- Anchors advance monotonically
- Prompt serialization stable and bounded
- Large tool outputs become handles (never inline)
notes: Implemented: ascc.rs module with CheckpointRecord lifecycle (from_frame, update_from_frame, revision tracking), FocusState→AsccSections conversion, 3 serializers (to_string_compact, to_messages_slots, to_digest), daemon checkpoint management (create/update on FocusStateUpdated), all 3 prompt assembly callers now pass AsccSections. 122 tests pass. Committed: fd42b42.

title: FrameRecord: update FrameStats on turns, store completion_reason and completed_at
description:
G1-detail-05 defines FrameStats (MVP minimal): turn_count (u64), last_turn_id (Option<String>), last_token_estimate (Option<u32>). These must be updated as turns flow through the proxy.

G1-detail-05 UPDATE "Frame Completion Semantics" adds to FrameRecord: completed_at (Option<timestamp>), completion_reason (Option<FrameCompletionReason>). The UPDATE states: "Completion reason MUST be reflected in: recent_results, or failures (if error/blocked)." And: "Completion reason contributes signals: blocked → raises surface pressure on related candidates. abandoned → suppress related candidates."

CODE STATUS:

- last_turn_id: always None
- last_token_estimate: always None
- completed_at: NOT A FIELD on FrameRecord (spec UPDATE requires it)
- completion_reason: exists as FocusaEvent::FocusFrameCompleted field but NOT stored on FrameRecord after completion (reducer sets status to Completed but discards the reason)

title: ECS auto-externalization: enforce threshold on turn content

G1-detail-08 "Threshold Policy (MVP)" states: "Config: ecs.externalize_bytes_threshold default 8KB, ecs.externalize_token_estimate_threshold default 800 tokens. If either exceeded, externalize."

title: Intuition Engine: implement missing signal sources (consistency, repeated edits/tools)
description:
docs/05-intuition-engine "Signal Sources (MVP)" defines 4 categories:

1. Temporal Signals: "Frame duration exceeds expected bounds. Prolonged inactivity." — IMPLEMENTED (observe_stack checks frame duration > 30min, observe_turn checks inactivity > 5min)

2. Repetition Signals: "Repeated errors. Repeated edits. Repeated tool invocations." — PARTIAL (error string matching exists: "error", "Error", "panic", "failed". Repeated edits NOT detected. Repeated tool invocations NOT detected.)

3. Consistency Signals: "Contradictory decisions. Drift between stated intent and actions." — NOT IMPLEMENTED

4. Structural Signals: "Deep stack nesting. Frequent frame switching." — PARTIAL (deep nesting > 3 frames detected. Frequent frame switching NOT detected.)

Also from docs/05: "Aggregation: Signals are aggregated by type, related frame, time window. Aggregation produces: cumulative pressure, summarized description." — EXISTS in aggregation.rs but never called (covered by focusa-syh.1).

CODE STATUS:

- IntuitionEngine.observe_stack(): detects deep nesting + long-running frames — CORRECT
- detect_error_repetition(): fires at threshold (3 errors, every 3rd after) — EXISTS

- No tracking of edits or tool invocations
- No contradictory decision detection
- No frequent frame switching detection

SPEC INVARIANTS (exact):
- "Runs asynchronously only"

- "Emits signals, not commands"
- "All signals are explainable"

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):

- No runaway pressure
- Fully explainable output

title: Expression Engine: pass ASCC sections and parent context to prompt assembly
description:
G1-detail-11 "Slot-Based Structure (Canonical)" defines 7 slots. Slot 3 (Active Focus Frame) spec: "Serialize ASCC slots for active frame." Slot 4 (Parent Context): "Include up to N ancestors (default 2). Rules: include only intent, decisions, constraints. Omit artifacts unless explicitly required."

G1-detail-05 "How HEC Influences Prompt Assembly": "Prompt assembly always includes: Active frame ASCC checkpoint slots. Optionally includes parent ASCC checkpoints (bounded by budget). Never includes siblings or unrelated frames in MVP. HEC provides focus proximity: active = 0, parent = 1, grandparent = 2. Used by prompt assemblers inclusion heuristics."

CODE STATUS:
- assemble_from() accepts AssemblyInput with ascc, parent_frames, rules, handles — EXISTS, CORRECT
- build_focus_slot() renders ASCC or FocusState — EXISTS, CORRECT
- build_parents_slot() renders up to MAX_PARENT_FRAMES (2) ancestors — EXISTS, CORRECT
- build_handles_slot() renders handle references — EXISTS, CORRECT

BUT all callers pass incomplete input:
- openai::process_request() passes ascc: None — always
- anthropic::process_request() passes ascc: None — always (inferred from same pattern)
- turn.rs prompt_assemble passes ascc: None — always
- All callers pass parent_frames: &[] — never builds ParentContext from stack

The engine is correct but starved. Depends on focusa-syh.3 (ASCC instantiation) and focusa-syh.2 (Focus State population).

G1-detail-11 also specifies "Delta Injection Rule": "Prompt Assembly must reuse prior assembled prompt context, inject only changes since last turn where possible." — NOT IMPLEMENTED (each turn builds full prompt from scratch).

ACCEPTANCE (spec exact):
- Prompt size stabilizes across turns
- No duplicated ASCC content
- Handles never inline by default
- Budget enforcement deterministic
notes: Implemented build_parent_contexts() walking parent chain (max 2 ancestors). All 3 callers switched from assemble() to assemble_from() with parents + frame title. Zero warnings, 122 tests pass. Committed: 0a5a7d0.

title: Memory: emit events for mutations (semantic upsert, rule reinforce, decay)
description:
G1-detail-03 §Event Log: "every state mutation must emit an event." G1-detail-15 §Memory events: memory.semantic_upserted, memory.rule_reinforced, memory.decay_tick.

core-reducer.md §Canonical Event Types lists Memory actions: UpsertSemantic, ReinforceRule, DecayTick. These should go through the reducer as events.

CODE STATUS:
- daemon.rs translate_action for UpsertSemantic: directly calls memory::semantic::upsert() then persistence.save_state() — BYPASSES REDUCER, no event emitted
- daemon.rs translate_action for ReinforceRule: directly calls memory::procedural::reinforce() then persistence.save_state() — BYPASSES REDUCER, no event emitted
- daemon.rs translate_action for DecayTick: directly calls decay functions — BYPASSES REDUCER for memory (gate decay does go through state mutation but no event)
- No FocusaEvent variants for memory mutations (MemorySemanticUpserted, MemoryRuleReinforced, MemoryDecayTick not in FocusaEvent enum)
- core-reducer.md pseudocode lists these as reducer actions but types.rs FocusaEvent enum does not include them

This violates: "If a cognition change cannot be expressed as a reducer event, it does not belong in Focusa." (core-reducer.md §Final Canonical Rule)

Also from G1-detail-15 "Replay Invariant": "Focusa state MUST be reconstructible by replaying events in order." Memory mutations that bypass the event log break this invariant.

ACCEPTANCE:
- UpsertSemantic emits memory.semantic_upserted event through reducer
- ReinforceRule emits memory.rule_reinforced event through reducer
- DecayTick emits memory.decay_tick event through reducer
- All memory mutations appear in event log
- State is reconstructible by replaying events (replay invariant)
notes: Memory mutations now emit audit events to event log. 122 tests pass. Committed: 80a728d.

title: Pi CRITICAL: auto-push Focus Frame on session start
description:
CRITICAL: Auto-push Focus Frame when Pi session starts.

FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts (or focusa-pi-bridge/index.ts)
HOOK: session_start

IMPLEMENTATION:
  1. GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/focus/stack (2s timeout)
  2. If stack.active_frame_id exists → use existing frame, skip push
  3. If no active frame:
     a. Derive project name: ctx.cwd.split('/').pop()
     b. Check for beads: pi.exec('bd', ['ready'], {timeout:3000})
        If result: use first bead ID as beads_issue_id
        If no result: use 'pi-session-' + Date.now()
     c. POST http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/focus/push
        Body: {title: 'Pi: $projectName', goal: 'Work on $projectName', beads_issue_id: $beadsId, constraints: [], tags: ['pi', projectName]}
  4. On failure: log warning, continue without frame (degraded mode)

VERIFY: Start Pi in a project directory. curl :8787/v1/focus/stack shows new frame.
DEPENDS: Focusa daemon running. Extension skeleton exists (focusa-e1w).

  5. Read SOUL.md pillars: fs.readFileSync('/home/wirebot/clawd/SOUL.md', 'utf-8').slice(0, 500)
  6. Fetch wiki decisions: shell exec 'wb wiki search tag:decision --format json --limit 3'

  Assemble ~500 token context string and return:
    { systemPrompt: event.systemPrompt + '\n\n' + wbmContext }

SCOREBOARD_TOKEN: process.env.SCOREBOARD_TOKEN or read from /run/wirebot/scoreboard.env

FALLBACK: Each fetch individually wrapped in try/catch with 2s timeout.
  Failure = 'Source: unavailable' line, not a crash.

VERIFY: /wbm on → next prompt shows [WIREBOT MODE ACTIVE] block. /wbm off → block disappears.
DEPENDS: focusa-e1w (skeleton must exist)
REF: doc 44 §29, UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §9.9
notes:
Must include context budget check per §36.7. Use ctx.getContextUsage() to calculate available headroom. Truncate Focusa injection to fit. Without this, Focusa can push Pi into context overflow.

title: Pi: register focusa_decide/constraint/failure TOOLS for LLM
description:
Register focusa_decide/constraint/failure as Pi tools.

FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts
API: pi.registerTool()

3 TOOLS:
  1. focusa_decide: params {decision: string, rationale: string, alternatives?: string[]}
     Handler: POST :8787/v1/focus/update {decisions: ['$decision (because: $rationale)']}
     If wbm: also pi.exec('wb', ['memory', 'inject', 'DECISION: $decision...'])
     Push to localDecisions array for local shadow
     Return: '✅ Decision recorded: $decision'
     promptGuidelines: ['When you make a significant choice, call focusa_decide.']

  2. focusa_constraint: params {constraint: string, reason?: string}
     Handler: POST :8787/v1/focus/update {constraints: ['$constraint']}
     Push to localConstraints
     Return: '⚠️ Constraint recorded: $constraint'
     promptGuidelines: ['When you discover a constraint, call focusa_constraint.']

  3. focusa_failure: params {failure: string, impact?: string}
     Handler: POST :8787/v1/focus/update {failures: ['$failure']}
     Push to localFailures
     Return: '❌ Failure recorded: $failure'
     promptGuidelines: ['When something fails, call focusa_failure.']

All tools: 2s timeout on Focusa call. On failure: still push to local shadow + return success.
Use Type.Object from @sinclair/typebox for params.
Use promptSnippet: 'Record decisions, constraints, and failures for cognitive tracking'

VERIFY: Ask LLM to make a decision. It calls focusa_decide. curl :8787/v1/focus/stack shows decision.

title: Pi CRITICAL: define injection layering rules (prevent double-injection)
description:
CRITICAL: Define and enforce injection layering rules.

FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts
AFFECTS: All injection hooks

RULES (authoritative):
  before_agent_start → behavioral instructions ONLY (§35.2). Fires once per prompt. ALWAYS ACTIVE.
  context → live Focus State refresh (§33.2). Fires per LLM call. ALWAYS ACTIVE.
  before_provider_request → DISABLED. Mutually exclusive with context event. §33.5 superseded.
  session_before_compact → ASCC compaction summary (§33.1). Fires on compact only.

IMPLEMENTATION: In the extension, do NOT register a before_provider_request handler.
  Comment: // §36.6: before_provider_request DISABLED — using context event instead

  before_agent_start fires 1x (instructions)
  context fires 1x per LLM call (state)
  before_provider_request does NOT fire
  Focus State appears ONCE in the assembled prompt, not 2-3x

title: Event naming mismatch with spec
notes:
Per G1-detail-15 §Event Types: spec wants dot-notation like focus.frame_pushed, gate.signal_ingested. Current: snake_case like focus_frame_pushed. Impact: Cosmetic, event structure correct. Fix: Add serde rename or type mapping. Low priority.
KNOWN SPEC DIVERGENCE: Event naming uses snake_case (focus_frame_pushed) instead of dot-notation (focus.frame_pushed). 

Rationale:
- 40+ variants would need individual #[serde(rename)] attributes
- Current structure is functionally correct
- High change risk, low functional value
- Can be addressed at API boundary with mapping layer if needed

The event schema is otherwise compliant with G1-detail-15.
